channel.title: DefaultAzureCredential on Larry James Henry
channel.description: Recent content in DefaultAzureCredential on Larry James Henry

item.title: Keyless AI: Using Entra ID Managed Identities for Azure OpenAI
item.description: You followed the quickstart. You grabbed the key from the portal, pasted it into a .env file, and your app worked. Now that key lives on your laptop, in your CI/CD secrets, probably in a Slack message from six months ago, and quite possibly in a git log you haven’t checked. It does not expire. It grants full access to every deployment in your OpenAI resource. You aren’t doing it wrong — you just followed the path Microsoft laid out for onboarding, not production.
